results.title: CME Group Software Engineer I 2026 | Freshers Bangalore
highlights:
# CME Group Hiring Software Engineer I 2026 | Freshers Apply Online Bangalore
[...]
CME Group, the world’s leading derivatives marketplace, is conducting its off campus drive in 2026 for the role of Software Engineer I at its Bangalore office. This is a rare entry-level opportunity to work on technology that powers global risk management across futures, options, and OTC markets. As a Software Engineer I, you will be mentored to build secure, scalable, and reliable solutions that directly impact the financial ecosystem. For 2025 and 2026 batch graduates, this role offers a fast track into fintech engineering with a global leader.
[...]
| Company | CME Group |
| --- | --- |
| Role | Software Engineer I |
| Location | Bangalore – Bagmane Tridib, India |
| Experience | Freshers |
| Education | B.E / B.Tech / BCA / MCA (CS / IT / related) |
| Salary | Competitive (Industry standards for derivatives marketplace) |
[...]
CME Group is targeting 2025 and 2026 batch graduates with a B.E, B.Tech, BCA, or MCA degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related streams. While the official criteria are broad, the company expects basic proficiency in at least one programming language (Java, Python, C#) and SQL. A strong academic record (60% or above in 10th, 12th, and graduation) is often preferred but not explicitly stated. Candidates with internship projects in database modeling, unit testing, or financial technology will have an edge. The ‘related’ branch clause means even electronics or mathematics graduates with coding skills can apply, but computer science backgrounds are prioritized.
[...]
Conduct coding at small task level under guidance
[...]
The selection process for CME Group Software Engineer I typically begins with resume screening on the Workday portal. Shortlisted candidates receive an online assessment covering aptitude, logical reasoning, English, and a basic coding test (usually in Java/Python/SQL). Those who clear move to a technical interview focusing on programming fundamentals, data structures, SQL queries, and project discussions. The final round is a managerial/HR interview assessing cultural fit, communication, and long-term aspirations. The entire process may take 2-4 weeks. Candidates are advised to apply early as applications close once sufficient candidates are received.

A standout requirement in this role is basic database SQL and modelling. While many fresher roles ask for generic coding skills, CME Group specifically emphasizes database knowledge because their systems rely heavily on relational databases for trade settlement, risk analytics, and market data. Candidates who can demonstrate hands-on SQL skills (joins, indexing, query optimization) in their resume or during the interview will have a distinct advantage. Consider building a small project that simulates a trade database or a risk calculation engine using SQL and Python—this directly aligns with the role’s expectations.
[...]
2025 and 2026 batch B.E / B.Tech / BCA / MCA graduates in CS, IT, or related branches with basic programming and SQL skills are eligible. Entry-level engineers with a learning attitude are preferred.
[...]
Click the ‘Apply Now’ button to go to the official CME Group Workday careers page. Create an account, fill in your details, upload your resume in PDF, and submit. Apply as soon as possible as the role may close early.

results.title: CME Group is looking for Software Engineer in Test (SET II) - IT Campus
highlights:
CME Group is looking for Software Engineer in Test (SET II) - IT Campus

The Software Engineer in Test II (SET II) makes applications more testable by developing software to support testing efforts. SET II has good development skills; & is able to interface efficiently w/ all of the support groups, other QA groups, & dev groups in working independently with some oversight. Employee must be comfortable working as part of an Agile team. S/he must exhibit quick learning capabilities in picking up & mastering new concepts- business, technical & process oriented.

Skills & Software Requirements:Object Oriented principles preferably in Java, Oracle, Big Data, Scripting (Python, PERL, BASH)

##### Key Responsibilities

• Develops and implements designs for automated testing framework. Develops software for making API’s more testable.• Interface and communicate with internal teams.• Responsible for creating and maintaing SDLC artifacts and facilitating reviews.• Work with Sr and Lead SET in implementing automation opportunities and overcoming testability challenges.

### About CME Group

CME Group is the world’s leading derivatives marketplace. But who we are goes deeper than that. Here, you can impact markets worldwide. Transform industries. And build a career by shaping tomorrow. We invest in your success and you own it – all while working alongside a team of leading experts who inspire you in ways big and small. Problem solvers, difference makers, trailblazers. Those are our people. And we’re looking for more.

How to Apply? Click Here to Apply Now Candidate may be redirected to recruiters/ employers database!

Last updated on May 10, 2026

results.title: BLM Analyst | Hirevector
highlights:
BLM Analyst | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# BLM Analyst

Posted May 29, 2026

CME Group is the world’s leading derivatives marketplace, and they are seeking a BLM Analyst to support their growth agenda. The analyst will conduct market analysis, manage data sets, and collaborate with various departments to drive strategic business initiatives.

### Responsibilities

- Conduct thorough market analysis to drive strategic business initiatives
- Candidates will be expected to manage large data sets for analysis; be able to structure and process qualitative and quantitative data and help draw insightful conclusions
- Create data driven presentations for use in both internal and external environments
- Provide strong data and quantitative analysis to monitor business, product, and industry participants trends
- Work with different departments across the company on time sensitive data analysis which helps summarize business goals, initiatives, customer performance, etc
- Provide customer support on product specific inquiries and occasional external data requests
- Monitor the competitive landscape and identify potential growth opportunities
- Develop a strong understanding of CME. Equity products, fielding customer inquiries and acting as a liaison with CME teams such as Client Development & Sales, Corporate Development, GCC, Clearing, Marketing, Technology, Operations, and more

Skills

- Bachelor's Degree (BS/BA) required; Degree in Business, Finance, Engineering or Economics preferred
- Strong written and verbal communication skills
- Must have strong analytical skills, including expertise in Google or Microsoft Suite and data aggregation
- Tools such as Bloomberg, Tableau or experience handling large data sets using database software or programming languages a plus
- An understanding of the financial system and CME Group's markets, and particularly a strong desire to learn more
- Proven strong organizational skills
- Must have strong teamwork, interpersonal and communication skills, while managing multiple stakeholders and client needs
- Demonstrate a passion for financial markets with close attention to detail and unafraid to ask questions
- Proven self-starter on projects and a desire to constantly assess and improve processes

### Benefits

- Annual target bonus opportunity
- Broad-based equity program
- Comprehensive health coverage
- Retirement package that includes both a 401(k) and an active pension plan
- Highly competitive education reimbursement provisions
- Paid time off
- Mental health benefit

Company Overview

- CME Group is a diverse derivatives marketplace that manages risk and capture opportunities. It was founded in 1848, and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, USA, with a workforce of 1001-5000 employees. Its website is http://www.cmegroup.com. Company H1B Sponsorship

- CME Group has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 2 in 2025, 1 in 2024, 1 in 2023, 3 in 2022, 2 in 2021, 9 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Apply for This Job

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist – Natural Language Processing (NLP) – Vice President – Machine Learnin… – NLP People
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist – Natural Language Processing (NLP) – Vice President – Machine Learnin… – NLP People
[...]
At JPMorgan Chase, AI and technology promote our global operations with unmatched scale and speed. We invest over $18 billion annually in innovation, data leverage, and security to shape the future for our clients, communities, and employees. The Chief Data & Analytics Office (CDAO) accelerates our data and analytics journey, with the Machine Learning Center of Excellence (MLCOE) creating and deploying solutions for complex business challenges. By ensuring data quality and leveraging insights, the CDAO supports our commercial goals, enhancing productivity and risk management through AI and machine learning. The CDAO is also responsible for developing and implementing solutions that support the firm’s commercial goals by harnessing artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies to develop new products, improve productivity, and enhance risk management effectively and responsibly.
[...]
As a Machine Learning Scientist – Natural Language Processing (NLP) – Vice President, you will own the full lifecycle of developing and deploying machine learning solutions, from ideation to production. Acting as a leading voice within JPMC on all things Generative AI (GenAI), you will partner closely with all lines of business to innovate new solutions that drive transformational change for the bank. You will actively participate in our knowledge sharing community, representing your work inside and outside of the firm at leading industry conferences amongst peers and leaders in the space. We seek someone who excels in a highly collaborative, fast-paced environment, and holds a strong passion for machine learning to make a significant impact at a leading global financial institution.
[...]
Job responsibilities Research and develop state-of-the-art machine learning models to solve real-world problems and apply them to tasks involving Generative AI (GenAI) Act as a thought partner for JPMC leaders and help the business identify and implement new machine learning methods that deliver impact Drive cross-functional collaboration with multiple partner teams such as Business, Technology, Product Management, Legal, Compliance, Strategy, and Business Management to deploy solutions into production Lead firm-wide initiatives by developing large-scale frameworks to accelerate the application of machine learning models across different areas of the business
[...]
Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills PhD in a quantitative discipline, e.g., Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Mathematics, Operations Research, Optimization, or Data Science, OR an MS with at least 3 years of industry or research experience in the field Solid background in Generative AI (GenAI) and hands-on experience and solid understanding of machine learning and deep learning methods and toolkits (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, NumPy, Scikit-Learn, Pandas) Ability to design experiments and training frameworks, and to outline and evaluate intrinsic and extrinsic metrics for model performance aligned with business goals Scientific thinking with the ability to invent and to work both independently and in highly collaborative team environments Solid written and spoken communication to effectively communicate technical concepts and results to both technical and business audiences
[...]
Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills Strong background in Mathematics and Statistics; Familiarity with the financial services industries and continuous integration models and unit test development Knowledge in search/ranking or Meta Learning Experience with A/B experimentation and data/metric-driven product development, cloud-native deployment in a large-scale distributed environment, and ability to develop and debug production-quality code Published research in areas of Machine Learning or Deep Learning at a major conference or journal

results.title: Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People
highlights:
Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People

## Overview

JPMAM Data Science is dedicated to enhancing and streamlining every stage of the investment process, from financial analysis and portfolio management to client services and advisory. We analyse extensive collections of data including financial documents, analyst reports, news articles, meeting notes, and client communications to empower data-driven decision making and process automation. We are seeking passionate data scientists who can apply the latest methodologies to generate real-time and actionable insights.

The candidate must excel in working in a highly collaborative environment together with the business stakeholders, technologists, and control partners to deploy solutions into production. A strong passion for machine learning is essential, along with a commitment to continuous learning, research, and experimentation with new innovations in the field. Candidates should possess a solid understanding of modern NLP and/or financial knowledge, hands-on implementation experience, strong analytical thinking, and a keen interest in applying advanced analytics to solve complex problems in finance and asset management.

Job Responsibilities

Design and implement advanced techniques such as semantic search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), named entity recognition (NER), prompt engineering, and personalization for content extraction, search, question answering, reasoning, and recommendation. Develop LLM, NLP, and ML solutions that address client requirements and drive business transformation. Work closely with partner teams—including Business, Technology, Product Management, Strategy, and Business Management—to deploy and scale developed models in production environments. Build comprehensive testing setups to evaluate model performance and ensure efficacy and reliability. Communicate results effectively to business stakeholders through written reports, visualizations, and presentations. Stay current with the latest research in LLM, ML, and data science, and proactively identify and leverage emerging techniques to drive ongoing enhancement.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills

Advanced degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or Machine LearningProven experience applying NLP, LLM, and ML techniques to solve high-impact business problems. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and familiarity with machine learning libraries and frameworks. Excellent communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Strong analytical skills with an understanding of financial markets and asset management.

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities and Skills.

results.title: AI Scientist Job at CIBC Toronto | GenAI Role Hiring
highlights:
AI Scientist Job at CIBC Toronto | GenAI Role Hiring
[...]
# AI Scientist – Toronto, ON (Hybrid) | Generative AI, ML, Banking AI Role with Enterprise Impact
[...]
This AI Scientist role at CIBC (Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce) is a high-impact opportunity within the bank’s Advanced Analytics & AI division. The team focuses on building production-grade Machine Learning and Generative AI systems that power automation, document intelligence, fraud detection, and operational efficiency across enterprise banking. The ideal candidate is a STEM-trained professional with hands-on experience in Python, LLMs, and applied AI systems, capable of translating research concepts into scalable business solutions in a regulated financial environment.
[...]
This role is best suited for early-to-mid career AI practitioners who want to transition into enterprise AI engineering and applied research. You will collaborate with senior data scientists, ML engineers, and business stakeholders to deploy AI systems across real-world banking workflows.
[...]
- 💰 Official Salary: Not disclosed
- 📊 Estimated Range: CAD $95,000 – $155,000 (AI Scientist / Applied ML level in Canadian banking sector)
- 📈 Level: Mid-Level AI / Applied Machine Learning
[...]
This position is centered around building scalable AI systems inside a regulated banking ecosystem. Roughly 40% of the role focuses on building and optimizing Machine Learning models, including classification, prediction, and risk scoring systems. Another 30% is dedicated to Generative AI workflows, such as LLM orchestration, prompt engineering, and document understanding using OCR and NLP pipelines. Around 20% involves production engineering, including deploying models using Python, APIs (FastAPI/Flask), and cloud infrastructure (likely Azure). The remaining 10% is collaboration with business stakeholders across fraud, operations, and finance teams.
[...]
You will be expected to design AI automation pipelines that extract structured insights from unstructured documents, reduce manual processing overhead, and improve decision-making efficiency. A key expectation is translating research-level ML techniques into production-ready systems with reliability, scalability, and compliance in mind. Familiarity with frameworks like Scikit-learn, XGBoost, and modern LLM tooling will be critical for success.
[...]
- ✅ Must: Python programming (production-level)
- ✅ Must: Machine Learning fundamentals (classification, regression, statistics)
- ✅ Must: Generative AI / LLM experience (prompting, workflows)
- ➕ Bonus: Azure or cloud AI deployment experience
- ➕ Bonus: NLP, OCR, or document intelligence systems
[...]
You will design and deploy AI-powered solutions across multiple banking domains. Core responsibilities include developing Machine Learning models for prediction and classification, building Generative AI pipelines for document understanding, and implementing LLM-based automation workflows. You will also contribute to production systems using Python, APIs, and cloud services while ensuring scalability and compliance with financial regulations.
[...]
- 🎯 Best apply method: Apply early via official CIBC careers portal
- 🔥 Highlight: LLM / GenAI projects
- 🔥 Highlight: Production ML systems
- ❌ Avoid: Generic academic-only resumes
- ❌ Avoid: Missing deployment experience
[...]
You are a senior technical recruiter.Role: AI Scientist focused on Generative AI, NLP, and enterprise ML systems in banking.
[...]
GenAI Experience: Showcase LLM workflows, prompt engineering, document intelligence systems
[...]
ML Deployment: Demonstrate production ML models using Python, FastAPI, Flask, cloud
[...]
Banking AI Impact: Quantify fraud, risk, or operations AI improvements in percentages
[...]
NLP & OCR: Highlight text extraction, document parsing, and NLP pipelines built
[...]
ML Stack Tools: Include Scikit-learn, XGBoost, Pandas, and Azure experience clearly

results.title: Data Scientist for Emerging Markets at Commodity Futures Trading Commission
highlights:
Data Scientist for Emerging Markets at Commodity Futures Trading Commission
[...]
# Data Scientist for Emerging Markets at Commodity Futures Trading Commission
[...]
The Division of Data (DoD or Division) serves as the leading authority on developing, implementing, and operating the Commission’s data strategies. The Division is comprised of the Data Operations and Standards Branch (DOSB) and the Enterprise Data Analytics (EDA) section. This position is in the EDA section.
[...]
Assist senior Data Scientists and support the Chief Data Scientist (CDS) and Chief Data Officer (CDO) in developing, defining, and driving the CFTC’s data strategy to advance the exploration and deployment of AI, ML, and enterprise analytics for improved or novel outcomes across traditional and emerging market segments.
[...]
Assist in identifying and developing data-driven solutions for complex business challenges to expand the agency’s data analytics and computational research capabilities.
[...]
Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets from multiple sources for data quality, accuracy, and integrity.
[...]
Conduct exploratory data analysis on large, complex datasets containing structured and unstructured data to identify novel trends, patterns, relationships, and anomalies.
[...]
Utilize modern tools and computational skills to interpret, connect, predict and make discoveries in complex data and deliver recommendations for business and analytic decisions.
[...]
Develop predictive models that use advanced statistical and machine learning methods to strengthen the CFTC’s surveillance capabilities and achieve CFTC policy goals, including data-driven rules generation, market monitoring, examinations and surveillance. Assist in the development of AI and ML models for risk scoring and predictive measures to identify candidates for further investigation using structured (especially tabular), and unstructured data.

BASIC QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: To be considered for this position, you must have either:1. Degree: Mathematics, statistics, computer science, data science or field directly related to the position. The degree mut be in a major field of study (at least at the baccalaureate level) that is appropriate for the position.OR2. Combination of education and experience: Courses equivalent to a major field of study (30 semester hours) as shown in paragraph A above, plus additional education or appropriate experience.In addition to meeting the specialized experience below you must meet the basic requirement above to qualify for this position.Specialized experience for this position includes:CT-13: To qualify for the CT-13 level you must have at least one year of specialized experience equivalent to the next lower grade (GS/CT-12 level). Specialized experience is defined that through which one has gained experience:

Experience leading data science projects and providing technical guidance. AND
[...]
Experience in advanced statistical models, machine learning, big data technologies, and cloud-based analytics platforms. AND

Experience mentoring staff and presenting

Experience designing and implementing automated data science workflows and pipelines.

highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including
[...]
machine learning,

- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]

Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: Standard Chartered Data AI Specialist 2026
highlights:
Standard Chartered Bank is launching its 2026 recruitment drive for the role of Specialist, Data, Analytics & AI – OTCR, based in Chennai. This role is part of the bank’s AI Centre of Excellence (CoE) and focuses on transforming risk management and regulatory compliance through advanced analytics, machine learning, and generative AI. As financial institutions increasingly adopt AI to automate compliance, detect financial crime, and enhance credit risk modeling, Standard Chartered is seeking fresh talent with hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects. The position offers a unique opportunity to work on high-impact AI use cases in a regulated banking environment, bridging the gap between data science and business outcomes.
[...]
Candidates must hold a degree in a quantitative field such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Engineering. A postgraduate degree or professional qualifications in analytics, risk, or compliance are advantageous. While the role is open to freshers, hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects (even academic POCs or personal projects) is a strong differentiator. The bank values background in solution architecture, data/ML engineering, or advanced analytics. For freshers, relevant internships or capstone projects in financial crime, credit risk, or compliance analytics will give an edge. The bank typically looks for candidates with strong analytical thinking, programming proficiency (Python, R, SQL), and familiarity with ML lifecycle management.
[...]
- Design and deploy advanced analytics, ML, and AI solutions for risk management and regulatory compliance.
- Identify and prioritize high-impact AI/ML use cases including financial crime, credit risk, operational risk, and compliance monitoring.
- Build robust, explainable models that comply with internal policies and external regulations.
- Embed models into business processes, ensuring trust, monitoring, and continuous improvement.
[...]
- Python
- R
- SQL
- Machine Learning (supervised & unsupervised)
- Natural Language Processing
- GenAI/LLM
- MLOps
- Data Governance
[...]
The selection process typically includes an initial resume screening, followed by an online assessment covering quantitative aptitude, logical reasoning, and basic data science concepts. Shortlisted candidates then face a technical interview focusing on Python, SQL, ML algorithms, and GenAI/LLM project experience. A final HR round assesses fit with the bank’s culture and risk awareness. The entire process may take 2-4 weeks. Given the specialized nature, candidates with demonstrable project work in NLP or LLMs have a higher chance of progressing.
[...]
The job description explicitly asks for hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects. In 2025-26, banks are racing to adopt generative AI for document review, automated reporting, and customer interaction. As a fresher, even a small project—like building a chatbot for compliance FAQs or fine-tuning an LLM for summarising legal documents—can significantly boost your application. The bank is looking for candidates who understand not just how to use pre-trained models, but also how to evaluate their outputs for bias, accuracy, and regulatory compliance. results.title: Senior Financial Data Engineer | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Financial Data Engineer | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Senior Financial Data Engineer

Posted Jun 3, 2026

### About the position

Investment Management Fintech Strategies (IMFS) is a global team headquartered in Malvern, Pennsylvania. IMFS serves to actively explore new technologies in ways that could dramatically improve the Vanguard’s investment performance, make capital markets work better for our investors, and embed a culture of innovation throughout Vanguard. This team seeks out internal and external partners and prospects all aspects of technology for ways to experiment and disrupt securities investing across all asset classes. We focus on data strategies including new sources of data and advanced analytics capabilities including machine learning and artificial intelligence; platform and visualization strategies; and market structure strategies.

- Responsibilities
- Design and enhance proprietary data pipelines and market data platforms
- Lead the onboarding of new data products for research and hypotheses testing requiring large scale historical data
- Work closely with data scientists on implementation of AI/ML models
- Mentor junior engineers to develop production quality code, data pipelines and analytics tools
- Collaborate with product and technology teams to drive the integration of analytics tools into production systems
- Work on proof-of-concepts and prototypes involving ingestion of unstructured data into Generative AI tools
- Stay current on latest innovation in application of AI in financial markets

- Requirements
- Educational background in Computer Science, Engineering, or related discipline with excellent academic credentials
- Strong knowledge of multiple financial data domains
- Deep knowledge of database concepts with proficiency in SQL and stored procedures
- 7+ years of strong coding experience in Python, C++, C#, and SQL
- Experience in processing large and complex datasets
- Has intellectual curiosity and is highly motivated to learn application of new technologies
- Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams
- Self-starter, motivated, independent, curious, versatile team player

- Nice-to-haves
- Exposure to equities trading environment and domain knowledge in market microstructure a plus

Apply for This Job

highlights:
Quantitative Developer___ Chicago, IL (Onsite) Contract | Jobflarely

Jobflarely.← All Jobs

# Quantitative Developer___ Chicago, IL (Onsite) Contract

Posted May 25, 2026

Job Title: Quantitative Developer

### Location: Chicago, IL - 100% Onsite from day 1.

Long Term Contract - W2 / C2C

Job Summary: We are seeking a highly experienced and adaptable Quantitative Developer to join our team in Chicago. This role requires a unique blend of strong quantitative and technical skills, deep financial domain knowledge, and a proactive learning attitude. You will collaborate closely with quantitative researchers, risk managers, and portfolio management teams to design, develop, and optimize analytical tools and models in a high-performance computing environment.

- Key Responsibilities
- Design and implement production-grade code that translates quantitative models into efficient and scalable solutions.
- Work closely with Quantitative Research, Risk, and Equity Portfolio Management teams to support model development and risk analytics.
- Contribute across the software development lifecycle including requirements analysis, coding, testing, and deployment.
- Build solutions using a wide array of technologies including Python, PySpark, R, Java, and cloud-based big data platforms like Databricks.
- Develop in both real-time and batch-oriented architectures.
- Employ Test-Driven Development (TDD) to ensure code quality, scalability, and maintainability.
- Continuously explore and integrate modern technologies and industry best practices into development processes.
- Communicate complex quantitative and technical concepts effectively to non-technical stakeholders.
- Required Qualifications
- Education: Master's or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Mathematics, Financial Engineering, or a related quantitative field from a reputed institution.
- Experience:
- Overall 12+ years of IT experience.
- Must have at least 5-8+ years of progressive experience in software engineering and quantitative development.
- Technical Skills:
- Proficiency in Python and PySpark (must-have), with hands-on experience in R and Java.
- Strong experience with data processing libraries such as Pandas, Polars, CuML, etc.
- Familiarity with cloud big data platforms, particularly Databricks.
- Experience working with large datasets and building scalable data pipelines.
- Domain Knowledge: Solid understanding of financial instruments including securities and derivatives, along with capital markets structure.
- Development Practices: Strong commitment to clean code, adaptive systems, and iterative design using TDD methodologies.
- Soft Skills:
- Quick to learn new technologies and quantitative methods.
- Able to explain technical strategies and solutions to both technical and business audiences.

- Preferred Attributes
- Exposure to quantitative research and alpha modeling.
- Experience building risk engines or simulation frameworks.
- Familiarity with orchestration frameworks like Airflow or equivalent.
- Ability to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment with minimal supervision.

To Apply: Please share resume to ;

results.title: Senior Software Engineer, Quant - hybrid in Chicago, IL, Overland Park, KS or NYC | Wfh
highlights: as a global market leader. Our teams are empowered to share ideas, actively pursue them and bring on a challenge. As champions of internal mobility and access to opportunity, we encourage our people to “go for it” and equip our managers with the training to coach their teams to the next level. We strive to provide employees a safe space to network, share ideas and create opportunities. Sound like the place for you? Join us! Cboe is looking for an experienced Senior Quantitative Developer who seeks a new challenge in the financial technology services industry. You will work within a team of seasoned developers, technologists, and quants with immense experience in financial markets and a keen interest in financial modeling and analytics. Responsibilities: Develop and implement quantitative models and software applications to process and analyze real-time financial market data in a high-performance, real-time computing environment Maintain and optimize existing software applications, and recommend and implement improvements Write technical specifications, project plans, and technical documentation; translate business requirements into functional specifications and project plans Regular communication with senior managers and technical colleagues Processing, collecting, and analyzing financial market data, including high frequency real-time pricing data and reference data Monitor and improve the quality of analytical data generated from our applications; develop/work with reference data team to add or modify reference data sources Work with product managers and business development team to write documentation, technical specifications, project plans and sales material for our data and applications Experience: Minimum 5 years of experience in financial markets required (market data, reference data, risk) Quantitative STEM background with a strong interest in financial markets, derivatives pricing, quantitative modeling, and risk analytics experience Experience in financial derivatives modeling, corporate actions, securities, and derivatives reference data, including futures and options contracts Strong programming skills in Java and/or C++; functional programming skills in SQL; and working knowledge of R, Python/NumPy, MatLab, or similar language for working with data and performing scientific computing. CUDA programming experience preferred. Strong knowledge of and extensive experience with statistical concepts including Bayesian modeling and hypothesis testing; linear regression; principal components (PCA); tree models; and time series modeling such as GARCH Specific Skills Required: Strong attention to detail, a highly analytical and quantitative mind, capable of translating analytical insights into actions Ability to work as a self-starter with minimal supervision; take ownership of projects from start to finish; develop requirements and specifications; QA testing, documentation and production release Ability to multi-task, prioritize and manage time effectively, in a deadline driven environment Strong interpersonal and communication skills necessary to work within a global team Education: Bachelor's degree required and Master’s or PhD in a STEM field highly preferred Benefits and Perks of working for Cboe Global Markets We value the total wellbeing of our people – including health, financial, personal and social wellness. We believe standard benefits like health insurance and fair pay are a given at any organization. Still, you should know we offer: Fair and competitive salary and incentive compensation packages with an upside for overachievement Generous paid time off, including vacation, personal days, sick days and annual community service days Health, dental and vision benefits, including access to telemedicine and mental health services 2:1 401(k) match, up to 8% match immediately upon hire Discounted Employee Stock Purchase Plan Tax Savings Accounts for health, dependent and transportation Employee referral bonus

results.title: SVP, Chief Data Officer - flexible hybrid in Chicago, IL | Wfh
highlights: About the position Building trusted markets —powered by our people. At Cboe, we inspire our people to solve complex challenges together because what we do matters. We provide the financial infrastructure that powers the global economy. As a leading provider of market infrastructure and tradable products, Cboe delivers cutting-edge trading, clearing and investment solutions to market participants around the world. We’re building inclusive ways to support professional and personal development while strengthening the trust we’ve earned as a global market leader. Our teams are empowered to share ideas, actively pursue them and bring on a challenge. As champions of internal mobility and access to opportunity, we encourage our people to “go for it” and equip our managers with the training to coach their teams to the next level. Our Associate Resource Groups champion diversity, equity and inclusion, giving associates a safe space to network, share ideas and create opportunities. Sound like the place for you? Join us! We are seeking an influential, future-focused Chief Data Officer to architect the next generation of our enterprise data ecosystem. In this role, the SVP will shape the vision for how data and AI accelerate innovation, unlock new revenue opportunities, and enable intelligent decision making at scale. They will steward our global data platform, modernize data governance, and elevate a data first culture across every function. This leader will ensure data is trusted, accessible, and strategically activated to power advanced analytics and automation. Responsibilities • Set Vision & Strategy: Define and execute the enterprise-wide data strategy—encompassing streaming, at-rest, and cloud data—aligned with business goals and growth ambitions. • Lead & Inspire: Build and empower high-performing, diverse teams that thrive on collaboration, innovation, and continuous learning. • Drive Business Value: Partner with business and technology leaders to identify opportunities for innovation, operational excellence, and new revenue streams through data. • Champion Data Quality & Governance: Develop and own the data governance framework, ensuring robust quality, integrity, privacy, and ethical use across the enterprise. Ensure compliance with relevant global regulations • Accelerate AI Adoption: Lead the deployment of generative AI and support advanced analytics, embedding transformative solutions into core business processes. • Deliver Scalable Solutions: Architect and deliver the global data platform that supports business, regulatory, and client needs, enabling deep insights and product innovation. • Engage Stakeholders: Build strong relationships with internal teams, external partners, regulators, and vendors (e.g., Snowflake, Sigma, AWS) to advance Cboe’s data objectives. • Foster Data Literacy: Act as a catalyst for cultural change, embedding data-driven thinking and championing AI literacy across all levels of the organization. • Promote Engagement and Retention: Strengthen engagement and retention by setting impactful objectives, fostering innovation, and ensuring diverse perspectives are integral to our culture and the way we achieve our goals. • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with marketing, finance, operations, product, and other teams to deliver actionable insights and solutions. • Data Security & Access Controls: Define and manage data access controls, user permissions, and data security practices to safeguard sensitive information. • Metrics & KPIs: Establish a rigorous measurement discipline including KPI governance to ensure data investments drive measurable outcomes such as data quality and informed decision making across Cboe. • Strategic Focus: Lead Cboe’s data-driven future, empowering decision-making and delivering trusted intelligence platforms, AI solutions, and enabling actionable analytics. • Extend our Data Platform to include all Cboe data that unlocks

results.title: CME Group | Leveraging 13F Filings for Institutional Insights and Futures Market Opportunities | DSI
highlights:
CME Group | Leveraging 13F Filings for Institutional Insights and Futures Market Opportunities | DSI

CME group is one of the world’s leading derivatives marketplace allowing clients to trade futures, options, cash and OTC markets – empowering market participants to efficiently manage risk and capture opportunities. CME Group aims to explore how public 13F filings—quarterly reports submitted by institutional investors to the SEC—can be leveraged to generate actionable insights for sales, marketing, and product development. This project’s goal is to help CME better understand which firms are exposed to asset classes relevant to its futures products and how those exposures evolve over time.

Watch the team present this project at 02:36:55 in the session recording here.

Keywords: NLP, Futures, Time Series, SEC Form 13F, Risk Factor Hedging

### Faculty Advisor

# Roger Moore, MBA

Roger is an Associate Clinical Professor at the University of Chicago. He teaches Leadership Skills in the Master of Science in Applied Data Science program. Previously Roger was VP, Analytics and Customer Operations at Entytle. Entytle provides a SaaS tool supporting customer installed base automated sales with AI and machine learning. Roger has also worked at Gartner, Sagence, Booz & Co, PwC, Diamond Management & Technology Partners and the Boston Consulting Group. Roger is active in the data, analytics and technology communities in the Chicago area. He attends many MeetUps, roundtables and industry events. Roger founded and chairs the Chicago Booth Big Data & Analytics Roundtable.

results.title: Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York
highlights:
# Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate Job Opening in New York, New York - Crains New York
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative AI - Senior Associate
[...]
Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. You will contribute to technical design decisions, delivery planning, and continuous improvement of our platforms and products.Job responsibilities
[...]
- Build and operate production generative artificial intelligence services and reusable backend application programming interfaces for firmwide use
[...]
- Combine enterprise data assets with large language and multimodal models to deliver high-quality user experiences
[...]
- Design scalable
[...]
- Collaborate with cloud engineering and site
[...]
- Ensure responsible artificial intelligence practices, controls, and governance are embedded into delivery and operations
[...]

[...]
- PhD in a quantitative
[...]

development lifecycle best practices

- Experience designing and implementing pipeline workflows using directed acyclic graph frameworks (for example Kubeflow, DVC, or Ray)
